summary.intent: Address PR review comments on cron_tool.rs by refactoring duplicate rescue blocks into a shared helper, adding debug tracing, and fixing flat-param exclusions.
summary.outcome: All three review comments resolved: unified rescue logic into a `rescue_stringified_object(&mut Value)` helper with tracing, and excluded `"id"` and `"limit"` from flat-param rescue. Changes committed and pushed.

repo: tracing is a direct dependency in the tools crate — no feature gate needed, used directly as `tracing::debug!`
cron_tool.rs had both owned and mutable-reference variants of the stringified-object rescue logic that needed unification
flat-param rescue in cron_tool must explicitly skip 'id' and 'limit' keys

code.finding: Replaced `try_parse_stringified_object(Value) -> Value` owned helper and 3 inline rescue blocks with a single `rescue_stringified_object(&mut Value)` helper that also emits `tracing::debug!` when rescue fires
normalize_job_value and normalize_patch_value updated to use the new &mut helper

workflow: PR review comments were fetched via Bash/gh CLI and addressed in a single focused commit
Verified tracing usage patterns via grep before adding new tracing calls to confirm no feature-gate ceremony needed

friction: Needed multiple grep passes to confirm tracing dependency model (no feature gate) before confidently using tracing::debug!
